decision_points.description: Every digest uses the EcoRI + BamHI pair for directional cloning into pUC19.

citations.title: NEB Double-Digest Finder (EcoRI and BamHI)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor tool confirming EcoRI + BamHI as a standard directional double-digest pair.

decision_points.description: Digest uses a buffer that supports simultaneous EcoRI + BamHI activity at 100% efficiency.

citations.title: NEB Double-Digest Finder
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor chart listing CutSmart / rCutSmart / NEB 3.1 as the recommended buffers for EcoRI + BamHI double digests.

decision_points.description: Digest incubation is at least 60 minutes to achieve complete plasmid digestion.

citations.title: NEB Optimizing Restriction Endonuclease Reactions
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol recommending at least one hour of incubation for complete plasmid digestion.

decision_points.description: Digest is heat-inactivated to remove residual nuclease activity before ligation.

citations.title: NEB Heat Inactivation Reference
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB reference supporting heat inactivation of EcoRI and BamHI at 65 C.

decision_points.description: Ligation uses T4 DNA ligase, the canonical cohesive-end ligase for this workflow.

citations.title: Enzymatic breakage and joining of deoxyribonucleic acid. I.
citations.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
citations.tier_justification: Foundational peer-reviewed description of T4 DNA ligase.

decision_points.description: Ligation vector-to-insert molar ratio is within the 1:10 - 10:1 range.

citations.title: NEB T4 DNA Ligase Protocol (M0202)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol recommending a 1:3 vector:insert molar ratio and flagging the 1:10 - 10:1 range as the acceptable envelope.

decision_points.description: Ligation temperature is within the standard T4 ligase range (16-25 C).

citations.title: NEB T4 DNA Ligase Protocol (M0202)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol noting that T4 ligase ligations are typically run at 16 C overnight or at 22-25 C for shorter durations.

decision_points.description: Selection plates use the vendor-recommended 100 ug/mL ampicillin concentration.

citations.title: NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor product page listing 100 ug/mL ampicillin for selection.

decision_points.description: Colony PCR is run only on white colonies (blue colonies are vector-only background).

citations.title: The pUC plasmids, an M13mp7-derived system for insertion mutagenesis and sequencing with synthetic universal primers
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed canonical pUC blue-white screening paper establishing that blue colonies carry empty vector.

decision_points.description: Colony-PCR screening reaches at least 95% cumulative confidence before stopping.

citations.title: NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook of Statistical Methods: Binomial Distribution
citations.tier_justification: Official NIST reference for the binomial cumulative distribution used to derive the 95% screening-confidence stopping rule.

wrong_digest_buffer.canonical_diagnosis: The digest buffer was incompatible with simultaneous EcoRI + BamHI activity.
acceptable_variants: CutSmart, rCutSmart, or NEB 3.1 should have been used for the EcoRI + BamHI double digest.
The chosen buffer did not support both enzymes at full activity.
The digest did not use the EcoRI + BamHI pair required for this directional cloning workflow.

wrong_ligase.canonical_diagnosis: A non-T4 ligase was used; T4 DNA ligase is required for ATP-dependent cohesive-end ligation in this workflow.
acceptable_variants: Only T4 DNA ligase can ligate EcoRI/BamHI cohesive ends under these conditions.
The ligase chosen cannot ligate cohesive ends with ATP in this format.

citations.title: Enzymatic breakage and joining of deoxyribonucleic acid. I.
citations.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
citations.tier_justification: Foundational peer-reviewed description of T4 DNA ligase.

extreme_molar_ratio.canonical_diagnosis: Vector:insert molar ratio was outside the standard 1:10 - 10:1 range, so productive ligation was unlikely.
acceptable_variants: The molar ratio used was outside the recommended 1:10 - 10:1 envelope.
Vector:insert molar ratio was extreme relative to the 1:3 NEB recommendation.

citations.title: NEB T4 DNA Ligase Protocol (M0202)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol setting the recommended molar-ratio envelope.

no_heat_inactivation.canonical_diagnosis: Restriction enzymes were not heat-inactivated, leaving active nuclease that degraded the ligation.
acceptable_variants: Digests were not heat-inactivated, so residual nuclease activity likely degraded the ligation.
Heat inactivation was skipped, which compromises downstream ligation.

citations.title: NEB Heat Inactivation Reference
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB reference for heat-inactivation conditions.

screened_blue_background.canonical_diagnosis: Blue colonies were submitted to colony PCR even though they represent vector-only background in this screen.
acceptable_variants: Blue colonies should not have been screened because they are empty-vector background.
Screening blue colonies wastes reagents because blue colonies retain lacZ alpha and do not contain the insert.

citations.title: The pUC plasmids, an M13mp7-derived system for insertion mutagenesis and sequencing with synthetic universal primers
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed canonical pUC blue-white screening paper establishing blue colonies as vector-only background.
